kind: dense data-model fast path with dense output for ConvertElementType
lever_summary: ConvertElementType built a Vec<Literal> via per-element convert_literal. Added a dense fast path: dense source (F64/I64 slice) -> dense-output target (F64/I64/Bool exact dtype) via a tight typed loop into new_f64_values/new_i64_values/new_bool_values, bypassing input materialization + convert_literal + the Vec<Literal> output. Other targets / non-dense fall through to generic.
method: same-bench A/B; baseline = stashed generic convert_literal loop; normalized by eval/sort_calib_reduce_64k_i64 (ReduceSum, unaffected).

change.lever: eval_convert_element_type: dense source (as_f64_slice/as_i64_slice) x dense-output target (F64/I64/Bool) -> new_*_values typed loop. Added eval/convert_64k_f64_to_i64 bench + convert_element_type_dense_matches_generic_all_targets test (11 targets x f64/i64).

isomorphism.type: BIT-IDENTICAL values to convert_literal (F64->I64 v as i64, F64->Bool v!=0.0, I64->F64 v as f64, etc.); dense vs Literal-backed value-equal
isomorphism.validation: convert_element_type_dense_matches_generic_all_targets (NaN/inf/-0/extremes, all 11 targets, by bits) + 6 convert tests pass

note: Earlier input-only dense read was ~neutral (1.09x); the win is DENSE OUTPUT (eliminating the Vec<Literal> build + per-element convert_literal). I32/F32/F16/BF16/U32/U64/Complex targets stay generic (no exact-dtype dense storage).
